Title: Exsim Hospitality unit secures RM63.5mil interior design works contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Exsim Hospitality Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, Exsim Concepto Sdn Bhd (ECSB), has accepted a letter of award from Cosmopolitan Avenue Sdn Bhd for the design and build of interior works under a RM63.5 million contract.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the hospitality company said the works commence on July 9, 2026 and are scheduled for completion on Dec 31, 2026, with a defects liability period of 12 months commencing from the date of Certificate of Practical Completion of the completed works.
"ECSB shall carry out the contract works in accordance with the other supplementary terms and conditions set out in the letter of award," said the company.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar says Malaysia must adapt to new security threats
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia can no longer rely on conventional approaches to national security as emerging threats now extend beyond the military, police and enforcement agencies, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said the old understanding of security as the sole responsibility of security forces was no longer sufficient, as threats, particularly in cybersecurity, now reached every home, individual and student.
"The old understanding was that security was the responsibility of the security forces, the military, police and enforcement agencies.
"But today, it has entered every home, every person and every student," he said in his keynote speech at the opening of National Security Month at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre today.
Present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and National Security Council director-general Datuk Raja Nushirwan Zainal Abidin.
Anwar said cybersecurity had become a central part of national security, alongside emerging fields such as artificial intelligence, quantum technology and cryptography.
He said Malaysia must move quickly to understand and adopt new technologies as part of its national security preparedness.
He said the government had introduced the National Cryptography Policy and MYCryptography Action Plan, adding that such areas were unfamiliar decades ago but had now become necessary.
Anwar called for a whole-of-nation approach, saying security could no longer be treated as the responsibility of one ministry, agency or sector alone.
"There can no longer be compartmentalisation. We cannot say this is a private sector issue, this is a department issue, or this is a ministry issue," he said.
He said the NSC, ministries, agencies, civil service leadership, political leadership and the private sector must work together to respond to threats in what he described as post-normal times.
Anwar said Malaysia must also strengthen cooperation with other countries, including through government-to-government arrangements and partnerships with companies from China, Europe and other regions, to keep up with new security demands.
He said his
earlier discussions
with Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul today had touched on security issues, peace frameworks, border management, encroachment and smuggling.
"In a borderless world, if we do not have close cooperation with all countries, we will face problems," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aizo Group secures RM9mil road and drainage project from Xense Design
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aizo Group Bhd's subsidiary Aizo Construction Sdn Bhd has secured a RM9.09 million subcontract for road and drainage works in Cheras, Selangor.
The contract was awarded by Xense Design Sdn Bhd on behalf of Esan Juta Sdn Bhd for infrastructure works supporting a proposed transit-oriented development (TOD).
The development comprises three serviced apartment blocks with 2,796 units and related facilities.
The scope of works includes road and drainage infrastructure to support site accessibility, readiness and long-term functionality.
The project started on June 18, 2026 and is scheduled for completion on Aug 17, 2027.
Executive director Ahmad Rahizal Ahmad Rasidi said the contract reflects continued confidence in Aizo's capability to undertake infrastructure-related works.
"This award adds further momentum to Aizo's construction pipeline and reflects the market's continued confidence in our ability to deliver infrastructure-related works," he said in a statement.
Rahizal noted that while road and drainage works are often less visible than vertical construction, they are critical in ensuring developments are properly supported from the ground up.
"This contract also fits well with our strategy of pursuing commercially viable projects that can contribute positively to the company's earnings," he added.
The contract is expected to contribute to the group's earnings for the financial year ending June 30, 2027.
Aizo said it will continue to pursue construction and infrastructure projects that support Malaysia's urban development, connectivity and sustainability agenda, while maintaining disciplined project execution and cost efficiency.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN's disciplined campaign gave it an advantage, says analyst
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) enters polling day with the upper hand after running a more disciplined, organised and locally focused campaign than Pakatan Harapan (PH), according to Ilham Centre.
Its executive director, Hisommudin Bakar, said BN successfully anchored its campaign on the performance of caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, whose popularity and administration became the coalition's biggest electoral asset.
Ilham Centre is an independent polling think-tank organisation that conducts public opinion surveys, political research and policy studies. The centre has built a reputation for producing some of the most accurate forecasts in previous general and state elections through extensive field surveys.
He said BN consistently projected a narrative centred on continuity, political stability and Johor's economic transformation, backed by achievements including the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), data centre investments and various state government cash assistance programmes.
"BN appeared to be ahead in terms of campaign acceptance because it built its message around Onn Hafiz's performance and administrative record.
"The coalition's campaign was disciplined, well-organised and rooted in local issues that voters could easily relate to," Hisommudin told the New Straits Times today.
Hisommudin said political exchanges between BN leaders and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim generated significant attention on social media platforms such as TikTok and Facebook, but failed to gain meaningful traction among grassroots voters.
Instead, he said, bread-and-butter issues remained the dominant concerns throughout the campaign.
"Cost of living, cross-border mobility with Singapore, affordable housing and worsening traffic congestion were the issues voters consistently raised regardless of age, race or gender," he said.
In contrast, Hisommudin said PH struggled to establish a defining campaign theme capable of capturing voters' attention.
Unlike previous elections, when PH successfully rode national issues such as the Goods and Services Tax (GST) and the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal, the coalition found it difficult to translate its role in the federal government into a compelling Johor-centric narrative, he said.
"The state election setting made it difficult for PH to create a wave around any particular issue. In our field studies, many voters could not clearly identify PH's main campaign message," he said.
He noted that PH Puteri Wangsa candidate Maszlee Malik's challenge to Onn Hafiz for a public debate on policies and governance failed to materialise, denying the opposition a potentially high-profile campaign moment.
Hisommudin said PH instead focused on themes of integrity and accountability while questioning who would ultimately benefit from Johor's rapid economic growth, particularly in relation to housing affordability and the spillover effects of Singapore's economy.
[[nid:1484636]]
Meanwhile, he said DAP intensified efforts to rally its traditional support base by reviving the narrative that "a vote for BN is a vote for Najib", amid concerns over voter enthusiasm.
Overall, Hisommudin said BN's campaign machinery proved more effective, with candidates beginning groundwork earlier, a coordinated election operation and the strong personal appeal of Onn Hafiz.
"Our field survey found Onn Hafiz enjoys a popularity rating of 79 per cent, which is exceptionally high and reflects Johoreans' recognition of his performance and efforts in leading the state," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alpha IVF raises stake in Singapore's unit for RM3.57mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Alpha IVF Group Bhd has raised its stake in Alpha International Women's Specialists (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. (AIWSS) to 96.53 per cent after subscribing for additional shares worth S$1.15 million (RM3.57 million) through a rights issue.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the fertility healthcare provider said it subscribed for 114.77 million new shares in AIWSS at S$0.01 per share using internally generated funds.
The subscription raised Alpha IVF's shareholding in AIWSS from 76.51 per cent to 96.53 per cent, with its total holding increasing to 129.43 million shares.
AIWSS offered 150 million new shares to its existing shareholders via a rights issue on June 12, 2026. Following the exercise, 35 million rights shares remained unsubscribed.
The Singapore-based unit operates a women's specialist centre and provides fertility treatments, women's healthcare and related medical services.
Alpha IVF said the additional investment will strengthen AIWSS's working capital, support operational expenditure and ensure sustainable cash flow in line with the group's strategic objectives.
The company said the subscription price was determined on a willing-buyer willing-seller basis, reflecting AIWSS' agreed valuation and funding requirements.
"The right issues subscription will enable AIWSS to fund its working capital requirements, operational expenditure and maintain sustainable cash flow," it said.
The investment is not expected to have a material impact on Alpha IVF's earnings, net assets, gearing or shareholders' equity for the financial year ending May 31, 2027, but it is expected to contribute positively to future profitability and earnings per share.
The transaction does not require shareholders' or regulatory approval as the highest applicable percentage ratio under Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market Listing Requirements is 1.88 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hartanah Kenyalang bags RM283.9mil project from JKR Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hartanah Kenyalang Bhd's unit Hartanah Construction Sdn Bhd has secured a RM283.9 million contract from the Public Works Department (JKR) Sarawak to build Wisma JKR Sarawak in Kuching.
In a bourse filing, the property developer and construction company said the project is the largest contract secured by Hartanah Construction to date.
Construction is scheduled to begin on July 23 and is expected to be completed within 30 months.
Hartanah said the contract is expected to contribute positively to the company's earnings and net assets for the financial year ending Oct 31, 2026, and throughout the construction period.
The company said the contract will not affect its share capital or shareholding structure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iraq will not leave Opec but seeks fair output quota, PM says
Article:
BAGHDAD: Iraq will not leave Opec, but is seeking a fair output quota within the oil producer group, Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi  said in an interview with Saudi-owned Al Arabiya TV, according to his office.
Reuters reported in an exclusive in June that Iraq, the group's second-largest producer after Saudi Arabia and one of its five founding members, had considered leaving Opec if the group did not allow Baghdad to ​significantly increase oil production.
Meanwhile, the United Arab Emirates raised its crude output to near record highs above 3.8 million barrels per day in June after it quit OPEC to escape production caps, two people familiar with production data said on Monday.
Saudi Arabia has set the official selling price for its flagship Arab Light crude to Asia in August at US$1.50 a barrel below the Oman/Dubai average, marking the biggest monthly cut in the price since Reuters records began in 2003.
Abu Dhabi National Oil Company has also been selling crude through tenders at discounted prices, traders told Reuters.
"It is increasingly looking like the Gulf producers are gearing up for a price war," said Robert Yawger, director of energy futures at Mizuho.
The Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies including Russia agreed on Sunday to further increase output targets by 188,000 bpd from August, on top of similar increases for June and July.
However, these increases have remained largely on paper because of the Iran war, which closed the Strait of Hormuz to tanker traffic for key Opec producers, including Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and Iraq, capping their output.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia rolls out B50, moves to end diesel imports
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia has officially launched its mandatory B50 biodiesel programme today, which the government said would enable the country to eliminate diesel imports for the first time.
Energy and Mineral Resources Minister, Bahlil Lahadalia, said Indonesia consumes between 38 million and 40 million kilolitres of diesel annually and previously imported around three million to four million kilolitres a year before the introduction of B50.
"By moving from B40 to B50, we can retain Rp170 trillion in foreign exchange, while reducing our imports further,"he said during the official rollout of B50 by President Prabowo Subianto in Karawang, West Java.
B50 is a diesel fuel blend containing 50 per cent biodiesel derived from crude palm oil (CPO) and 50 per cent petroleum diesel, up from the previous B40 blend, as part of Indonesia's efforts to reduce reliance on imported fossil fuels and increase the use of renewable energy.
Bahlil said B50 underwent six months of testing on trains, passenger cars, buses and ships, including vehicles from both Asian and European manufacturers, before being rolled out nationwide.
"The test results showed that the quality of B50 is much better than B40. Under B40, fuel filters typically had to be replaced after around 10,000 to 20,000 kilometres. With B50, some vehicles have reached 45,000 kilometres without requiring a filter replacement," he said.
He said the programme would increase domestic crude palm oil (CPO) demand from 15.2 million tonnes to between 16.3 million and 17 million tonnes annually, helping provide greater market certainty for the country's oil palm farmers.
Bahlil said the programme is also expected to increase the value-added of Indonesia's CPO industry from Rp20.92 trillion to Rp23.49 trillion while expanding employment from about 1.8 million workers under the B40 programme to 2.1 million under B50.
He said B50 would also increase greenhouse gas emissions reductions from 39.66 million tonnes of carbon dioxide to around 44.46 million tonnes.
Looking ahead, Bahlil said the government plans to introduce a mandatory ethanol-blended gasoline programme in 2027, starting with a blend of between 10 and 20 per cent using feedstocks such as sugarcane, cassava and corn.
He said B50 would be fully implemented nationwide after a two-month transition period, highlighting that about 56 per cent of diesel currently in circulation has already switched to the new blend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Bersatu denies 120 Pulai members have switched support to PH
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pulai Bersatu has denied claims that more than 120 of its members and leaders have declared support for Pakatan Harapan, describing the allegation as political propaganda ahead of the Johor polling day.
Pulai Bersatu acting division chief Che Wan Farid Che Wan Abd Rahman said those who made the declaration were no longer party members and some had left Bersatu, contested as independent candidates or joined other political parties.
He said others were no longer involved in the division's administration or activities.
"What was shared was merely propaganda aimed at attracting public attention during the Johor polls.
"We are disappointed that false information was published by a portal and urge them to remove the report," he said in a statement.
Che Wan Farid said the allegation was a negative tactic that could affect Bersatu's image, adding that claims the party had neglected its members were unfounded.
He said Bersatu had continued to carry out welfare programmes, community services and assistance initiatives for members and the public within its capabilities.
Meanwhile, Pulai Bersatu information chief Shamsudin Baharon said the claim that 120 Bersatu members had backed PH was not supported by verifiable evidence and appeared to be an attempt to create a negative perception of the party.
"It is inappropriate for them to use Bersatu's name to create the impression that they still represent the party's voice and position," he said.
Shamsudin said Pulai Bersatu remained confident that Perikatan Nasional's support base would remain intact and would not shift towards PH candidates.
He urged all parties to practise mature and ethical politics and refrain from misusing party names for political interests.
A portal had reported yesterday that 120 Bersatu members, including six former leaders and committee members at division and branch levels, had openly declared support for PH's Kempas candidate Muhammad Faezuddin Mohd Puad.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar raises concern over racial issues in Johor polls
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned against Malaysia remaining trapped in old racial and regional narratives, saying such polemics were worrying as Johor heads to the polls.
Without naming any party, Anwar said some issues being raised in Johor involving sentiments over districts and states were worrying.
"Sometimes old issues, such as race… look at the polemics (being played up) in Johor now, where sentiments over districts and states have become big issues that threaten the nation.
"This is something that needs to be looked at. For me, this is worrying," he said in his keynote speech at the opening ceremony of National Security Month at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre today.
Anwar said the country could not afford to wait for public narratives to change on their own, especially as Malaysia faced rapid shifts in technology, security and global risks.
"Should we wait until the narrative changes? No. That is the role of leadership in departments, agencies and ministries," he said.
He said government leaders must quickly grasp new technologies and emerging challenges, instead of remaining tied to old approaches.
The Johor election will see Anwar's coalition, Pakatan Harapan, contest against its federal government partner Barisan Nasional across all 56 state seats.
Early voting was completed on Tuesday. Polling day is on Saturday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PTT Synergy to sell land in Klang for RM17mil to improve finances
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PTT Synergy Group Bhd plans to sell a freehold industrial property in Bandar Bukit Raja, Klang, for RM17 million  to cut costs and improve its financial position.
The property will be disposed of by the company's indirect wholly owned subsidiary PTT Assets Sdn Bhd to WDG Resources Sdn Bhd under a sale and purchase agreement.
The asset comprises a 4,987-square-metre freehold industrial land with a one-and-a-half-year-old double-storey detached factory offering a gross built-up area of about 20,197 sq ft.
PTT said the disposal consideration was arrived at on a willing-buyer willing-seller basis.
The company expects to record a gain of RM2 million from the transaction, based on the property's carrying value of RM15 million as at June 30, 2025.
Of the RM17 million proceeds, RM11 million will be used to repay bank borrowings secured against the property, while the remaining RM6 million will be allocated for working capital, including operating expenses, staff costs and settlement of trade payables.
PTT said the disposal forms part of its efforts to rationalise non-core assets, while reducing operational and maintenance costs associated with the property.
The company noted that the proposed disposal is not expected to have a material impact on its earnings, earnings per share, net assets or gearing for the financial year ending June 30, 2027, apart from the one-off disposal gain.
The property, classified for light industrial use, was acquired in December 2022 at an original investment cost of RM11.28 million and currently carries an outstanding bank borrowing of RM11 million, which will be redeemed before completion of the transaction.
Under the agreement, the purchaser will pay a 10 per cent deposit upon signing, with the remaining 90 per cent payable within three months.
A one-month extension may be granted, subject to an interest charge of eight per cent per annum on the outstanding balance.
The proposed disposal is expected to be completed by the first quarter of PTT's financial year ending June 30, 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes flat as FOMC minutes weigh on US dollar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  The ringgit closed flat against the US dollar after the latest US Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes suggested a less unified stance on further policy tightening, an analyst said.
At 6pm, the ringgit was traded at 4.0750/0795 against the US dollar from 4.0750/0800 at Wednesday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the minutes weighed on the greenback amid expectations that the US central bank may keep interest rates unchanged for the remainder of the year, helping the local currency hold its ground.
Currently, the US federal fund rates stood at between 3.50 per cent and 3.75 per cent.
Besides, he said the latest decision by the Bank Negara Malaysia's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to keep the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) steady at 2.75 per cent seems to be ringgit-positive.
"The accompanying statement suggests that the MPC members are keen to keep the OPR steady at 2.75 per cent throughout the year with gross domestic product (GDP) growth projection of between four per cent and five per cent for 2026 is highly attainable.
"This also reinforced the view that the OPR will remain stable for 2026," Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.
At the close, the ringgit traded lower against a basket of major currencies.
It fell against the British pound to 5.4654/4714 from 5.4369/4435 at Wednesday's close, weakened against the euro to 4.6581/6633 from 4.6471/6528 and slid against the Japanese yen to 2.5091/5120 from 2.5083/5114 yesterday.
Against regional currencies, the local note traded mixed.
It fell against the Singapore dollar to 3.1518/1558 from 3.1487/1528 at Wednesday's close and slipped against the Thai baht to 12.1886/2064 from 12.1685/1882 previously.
The ringgit, however, appreciated against the Indonesian rupiah to 224.8/225.1 from 226.2/226.5 and edged up against the Philippine peso to 6.61/6.62 from 6.62/6.64 at Wednesday's close.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested, drugs worth over RM450,000 seized in Penang
Article:
NOBONG TEBAL: Penang police have crippled a liquid syabu processing and distribution syndicate with the arrest of a 40-year-old man and the seizure of drugs worth more than RM450,000.
The suspect was arrested at 9pm on July 6 during an operation by the Penang Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) in Seberang Prai Selatan.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim said police also seized about 9kg of liquid syabu, estimated to be worth more than RM450,000, during the operation in Nibong Tebal.
"Investigations found that the syndicate had been actively processing and distributing the drugs in the Nibong Tebal area since May this year," he said in a statement today.
Although the suspect tested negative for drugs in an initial urine screening, police checks revealed that he had previous records involving drug-related and other criminal offences.
Police also seized assets worth about RM70,000 under the Dangerous Drugs (Forfeiture of Property) Act 1988.
The suspect has been remanded for seven days from July 7 until July 13 to facilitate investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment and not fewer than 12 strokes of the cane upon conviction.
Lim said the success was the result of continuous intelligence gathering by the NCID and cooperation from the public.
He said police would not compromise with anyone involved in drug trafficking, including those found possessing, distributing or protecting drug-related activities, stressing that such offences were viewed seriously under the law.
He also urged the public to continue assisting police by channelling information on drug-related activities through the NCID hotline at 012-208 7222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jaecoo J8: Big SUV at disruptive price point
Article:
THE Jaecoo J8 enters Malaysia as one of the newer challengers in the crowded D-segment SUV space, but it does so with a clear strategy: offer size, luxury features and technology that punch above its price bracket.
It is not trying to reinvent the SUV formula. Instead, it focuses on something more straightforward - giving buyers a large, well-equipped, premium-feeling SUV at a price that undercuts many established Japanese and European rivals.
At a starting price of around RM178,800-RM198,800, the J8 positions itself as an "attainable luxury" SUV and on paper, it comes loaded enough to justify that claim.
Commanding Presence
The Jaecoo J8 immediately stands out due to its proportions. At 4,820mm long with a 2,820mm wheelbase, it has genuine road presence rather than compact-SUV mimicry.
Its styling is deliverately conservative in a premium sense. The large waterfall grille, slim LED lighting signature and clean surfacing give it a confident, almost executive SUV look.
It avoids overly aggressive design cues, instead leaning towards a more global, upscale aesthetic.
This is important because it signals Jaecoo's intent - the J8 is not positioned as a "budget alternative," but as a legitimate premium contender.
Powertrain
[[nid:1484599]]
Under the bonnet, the J8 is powered by a 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol engine, producing around 246hp and 385Nm of torque, paired with an automatic transmission.
Two drivetrain options are offered: 2WD (five-seater) and AWD (six-seater with torque-vectoring capability)
Performance is more than adequate for a large SUV of this size, with the AWD version offering better traction and confidence in wet or uneven conditions.
The engine is tuned more for smoothness and refinement than outright sportiness, which suits its positioning.
Acceleration is progressive rather than aggressive, and the gearbox prioritises comfort in daily driving.
Interior
The cabin is where the J8 makes its strongest impression. It leans heavily into technology and comfort, with a layout that feels more premium than its price suggests.
Key features include:
* Dual 12.3-inch digital displays (instrument cluster and infotainment)
* Sony premium audio system (up to 14 speakers in higher spec)
* Nappa leather upholstery (AWD variant)
* Ventilated, heated and massage front seats
* Panoramic sunroof
* Head-up display
* 540-degree camera system
* Wireless charging and multiple USB ports
* Power tailgate and smart entry system
The AWD variant elevates comfort further with "queen seat" style front passenger leg support, rear captain-style seating and additional luxury touches.
Material quality is generally impressive for the segment, with soft-touch surfaces in key areas and a design that prioritises space and comfort.
[[nid:1484598]]
Practicality
Practicality is another strong point. The J8's size translates into generous interior space, especially for rear passengers.
The 2WD version offers a conventional five-seat layout with a large boot, while the AWD variant provides a six-seat (2-2-2) configuration for more premium family use.
Boot space is competitive, and rear seat comfort is a standout, particularly for long-distance travel.
Technology And Safety
The J8 is equipped with a comprehensive suite of safety and convenience technologies. The 540-degree camera system is particularly useful in tight urban environments, while Level 2 ADAS features help reduce fatigue on highways.
The system includes:
* Adaptive cruise control
* Lane-keeping assist
* Blind spot monitoring
* Rear cross-traffic alert
* Autonomous emergency braking
While not segment-leading in every detail, the overall package is competitive and well-integrated.
Ride & Handling
On the road, the J8 AWD we tested feels most comfortable in relaxed cruising conditions. Ride quality is one of its strongest attributes, with suspension that absorbs bumps and broken surfaces well.
At highway speeds, it settles into a calm, stable gait, making it well-suited for long-distance travel. Cabin insulation is strong, with road and wind noise well controlled.
[[nid:1484597]]
Body roll is present in corners, expected for a large SUV, but it remains predictable rather than unsettling. Steering is light and tuned for ease of use rather than sharp feedback.
This is not a driver's SUV, but it is a very competent long-distance cruiser.
Verdict
The Jaecoo J8 is not trying to be the most dynamic SUV in its class. Instead, it focuses on delivering a premium experience at a significantly lower price point than traditional rivals.
Its strengths lie in comfort, space, features and presence. It feels more expensive than it is in many areas, particularly in the cabin experience and ride quality.
However, it is still a relatively new entrant in the market, and long-term considerations such as resale value, brand perception and after-sales maturity will be important for buyers to weigh.
That said, as a product, the J8 delivers a compelling proposition: a large, comfortable, well-equipped SUV that brings premium aspirations within reach of a broader group of Malaysian buyers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN candidates upbeat despite contesting in PH strongholds in Stulang, Perling
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: With one day left before polling on Saturday, Barisan Nasional candidates for the Stulang and Perling state seats, Bong Seng Heng and P. Pannir Selvam, remain upbeat despite contesting in constituencies considered Pakatan Harapan strongholds.
Both candidates said they would continue working hard until the final day of campaigning tomorrow.
"We are still working hard because we are contesting in PH strongholds. The campaign will continue right until the last day," they said during the Bicara PRN Johor podcast.
MCA newcomer Bong said the past two weeks of campaigning had revealed a noticeable shift in voters' reception towards BN.
"This time, we can clearly see the difference in the way people respond to us when we greet them and appeal for their votes. They are more receptive, maintain better eye contact and are far more willing to engage with us," he said.
Bong, who served as a Johor Baru City Council councillor for four years, attributed the shift in voter sentiment to the Johor government's performance under Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
"Voters recognise the achievements of the state government under BN's leadership. The state's economic performance has benefited many people, including the Chinese business community, and this has strengthened their confidence that BN can continue bringing Johor to greater heights," he said.
Meanwhile, when asked whether the high early voting turnout of 94.49 per cent on Tuesday (July 7) would pose a challenge to BN, Bong said voters were now more mature and would make their own decisions.
"We have read many analyses and comments suggesting that a high early voting turnout would not favour BN. However, I believe voters today are more mature.
"Regardless of whether the turnout is high or low, it is the responsibility of every Bangsa Johor voter to come out and vote, and to make the right and informed choice," he said.
Bong is in a four-cornered contest for the Stulang state seat against Andrew Chen of PH, Lim Chin Eng of Perikatan Nasional and Stanley Tan of Parti Bersama Malaysia.
Stulang's electorate comprises about 53 per cent Chinese voters, 38 per cent Malays and five per cent Indians.
Meanwhile, MIC newcomer Pannir said voters in Perling were increasingly concerned about unresolved local issues, noting that many of the problems raised by residents had persisted for years without meaningful progress.
He said feedback received throughout the campaign showed that residents were more concerned about day-to-day local issues.
"From what I have seen, many of the issues raised by residents have remained unresolved for nearly 15 years.
"Not only do these problems still exist, but some have become worse. People are now looking for someone who can genuinely help resolve these local issues," he said.
Pannir, who previously served as a Johor Baru City Council councillor, said his experience in local government had given him a deep understanding of the constituency's issues and how they could be addressed.
"My background enables me to focus on practical solutions and work closely with local authorities, village heads and community leaders to improve residents' quality of life."
Pannir also shared questions he received from voters about BN's decision to field an MIC candidate in Perling, a seat previously contested by MCA.
"Many people asked why an MIC candidate was contesting a seat previously held by MCA. I explained that BN is not a race-based coalition. It is a coalition whose component parties work together and support one another.
"Following discussions between the national and state leadership, BN decided to field me in Perling based on the Bangsa Johor concept, where every community is united under one umbrella.
"I represent everyone; Malays, Chinese, Indians and all other communities in Perling," he said.
Perling's electorate comprises about 48 per cent Chinese voters, 37 per cent Malays and 14 per cent Indians.
In Saturday's poll, Pannir will face Tee Boon Tsong of PH and Boo Wei Han of Parti Bersama Malaysia in a three-cornered contest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Russian frustration rises as fuel crisis bites
Article:
IN Russia's grain belt, farmers fret they will not be able to harvest their crops as a fuel crisis sparked by Ukraine's drone attacks on oil refineries and depots disrupts daily life.
As Kyiv tries to pressure Moscow into making peace with strikes on the country's energy infrastructure, the resulting damage has squeezed oil-rich Russia's fuel supplies, leading to restrictions in most regions and growing public disquiet.
This has pushed drivers to crowd-source maps and trade tips about which stations have fuel and shorter lines.
The shortage has also frayed tempers, with drivers shown in footage on social media getting into fights as they wait to refuel.
In one video, titled 'The Ultimate Luxury 2026,' a man slowly pours petrol into his lawnmower from a jerry can and jokes: "What riches. Who can afford this now?"
Online searches for "how to siphon fuel" jumped to more than 9,300 by June 21 from 697 a month earlier, website iPhones.ru said, citing Yandex data.
Russia has repeatedly attacked Ukraine's own energy infrastructure since launching what it calls its special military operation in 2022, leaving large numbers of Ukrainians without power or heating during winter.
Evidence of the widening impact of Ukraine's campaign is uncomfortable for Russian authorities, who initially downplayed fuel shortages as localised bottlenecks.
One social media post highlights farmers in the fertile Black Earth region struggling to afford fuel for harvest, while another describes a farmer having to drive his combine harvester to a regular gas station after he was not allowed to fill a can.
Reuters could not independently verify the accounts. President Vladimir Putin acknowledged there were issues on Sunday and promised measures to stabilise the market.
He said it was particularly important to maintain fuel supplies to the agricultural sector "because the harvest depends on it."
His point man on energy, Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak, said on Wednesday that the problems were being addressed.
Reuters reported exclusively that Russia had begun seaborne gasoline imports from India and that Kazakhstan had agreed to supply 50,000 metric tons to Russia in July and August.
Even before the shortages escalated last month, Russians were feeling more pessimistic about economic conditions than at any time in the past 20 years, a poll showed this week.
Some areas are seeing basic services cut back. In the Zabaikalsky region, bordering China and Mongolia, authorities cancelled some bus routes and a waste-collection firm suspended services in four districts, citing fuel constraints.
"More scary is how much groceries will cost. All deliveries are done by road," one person posted on an article about the cuts on the website of regional news outlet Chita.ru.
More than 100 people "liked" the comment. As strikes continue, prolonged fuel shortages could erode public support for the war, which began in February 2022 with Russia's invasion of Ukraine and is now in its fifth year.
Waiting in line at a filling station in the southern city of Rostov-on-Don on Monday, Tatiana Sedykh told Reuters she was glad she used diesel.
"The line for gasoline is just insane ... I'm starting to think maybe I should begin walking to work."
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia closes lower amid renewed Middle East tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Thursday as renewed geopolitical tensions in Middle East weighed on investor sentiment.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) fell 5.97 points, or 0.36 per cent, to 1,677.64 from Wednesday's close of 1,683.61.
The benchmark index opened 2.62 points lower at 1,680.99, and moved between 1,676.18 and 1,683.80 throughout the session.
However, market breadth was slightly positive, with gainers leading losers 533 to 504, while 547 counters were unchanged, 1,112 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover slipped to 2.64 billion units valued at RM2.19 billion from 2.96 billion units valued at RM2.18 billion on Wednesday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said market sentiment was cautious after the United States launched a second consecutive day of strikes on Iranian targets to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, while revoking Iran's oil export waiver.
This signals the possibility of further escalation, raising concerns over global energy supplies, he said.
"Despite the weaker index performance, market breadth improved from yesterday, with gainers leading by a broad-based advance across financial services, consumer, telecommunications and industrial stocks, suggesting selective bargain hunting and underlying resilience in domestic equities.
"Meanwhile, Bank Negara Malaysia's (BNM) decision to maintain the overnight policy rate (OPR) at 2.75 per cent provided policy certainty, reinforcing confidence in the domestic economic outlook and offering businesses and investors greater clarity for investment and expansion decisions," he told Bernama.
Earlier today, BNM in a statement announced that its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has maintained the OPR at 2.75 per cent for the seventh consecutive period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Witness says 5 letters prepared for Jana Wibawa project bids
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A company director said she prepared five separate letters on behalf of five companies addressed to then prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin seeking projects under the Jana Wibawa programme, the High Court heard today.
Nepturis Sdn Bhd director and senior executive procurement Aliza Abd Malek said she prepared the letters on the instructions of businessman Lian Tian Chuan, better known as TC Lian.
Apart from preparing a letter for Nepturis, Aliza said she also prepared letters for Permata Rebana Sdn Bhd, Fastcoll Corporation Sdn Bhd, JM Letrik Sdn Bhd and MZ Hakujaya Sdn Bhd, companies owned by Lian's business associates.
Aliza was testifying in Muhyiddin's trial, where he faces four charges of using his position as prime minister and Bersatu president to obtain RM232.5 million in alleged gratification for the party, as well as three money laundering charges involving RM200 million.
Under cross-examination by Muhyiddin's lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, Aliza agreed that the preparation of five different letters was intended to show that the companies had no connection with one another.
Amer Hamzah: The contents, wording and paragraphs of the letters were all different even though they were prepared by you. The recipient will assume they came from different companies?
Aliza: Yes.
Nepturis is the company linked to charges faced by former prime minister Muhyiddin, who faces seven charges involving abuse of power and money laundering related to the Jana Wibawa project.
It was revealed during the trial that Nepturis had obtained overdraft facilities from Maybank in February 2022, including a RM1 million payment made to Bersatu.
Earlier, Aliza said she and Nepturis managing director Rizman Abdullah became directors and equal shareholders in 2018 after being asked by Lian to take over the company.
Aliza said Lian held no official position or shares in Nepturis, but they understood that he effectively had a 30 per cent interest in the company through funding provided by his company, Metrasys Sdn Bhd.
She said major decisions involving Nepturis required Lian's approval.
Aliza also revealed that her company secured a RM141 million tender under the Jana Wibawa programme for the construction of the Klang Utara District Police Headquarters in Selangor.
She said the Public Works Department issued a letter of intent on Dec 10, 2021, offering Nepturis the RM141 million contract, before issuing the letter of acceptance on Jan 12, 2022.
The trial before judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin continues tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heatwave linked to more than 5,000 deaths in Germany, RKI says
Article:
BERLIN: Germany has recorded an estimated 5,120 heat-related deaths so far this year, most of them in late June when weekly average temperatures far exceeded 20C, the Robert Koch Institute (RKI) for public health said on Thursday.
Around 4,270 of the deaths were among people aged 75 and older, the RKI said in a weekly report. More women than men died, mainly because they make up a higher share of the very old.
The German data add to a grim picture across Europe.
The EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service said in a bulletin on Thursday that Western Europe had its hottest June on record with an average of 20.74 degrees.
National authorities have reported more than 4,700 excess deaths during the June 20-28 heatwave in France, Belgium, Spain and the Netherlands.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Parliament to host inaugural fun run on July 25 to promote healthy lifestyle
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parliament, through the Malaysian Youth Parliament (PBMy) secretariat, will organise its inaugural Larian Cergas Parlimen fun run on July 25 to promote a healthy lifestyle among lawmakers and the public.
Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Datuk Dr Johari Abdul said the programme would serve as a platform for members of parliament to champion healthy living, particularly among young people.
He said the 5km run would start from the Parliament building, pass several landmarks including Tugu Negara, and finish back at Parliament.
"We want all members of the Dewan Negara and Dewan Rakyat to become influencers in promoting the importance of maintaining good health, not only among fellow lawmakers but also among young people and the wider community.
"In recent times, there has been worrying news about the health of elected representatives due to heavy work commitments and unhealthy lifestyles. Therefore, this programme is being organised to encourage healthy living," he said at the launch of the programme at the Parliament building here today.
Johari said the run is also open to members of the public. Those interested in taking part can obtain further information and register via the official registration link.
Meanwhile, Johari expressed hope that following its debut at Parliament, the programme would also be organised at state legislative assemblies nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Travel agent gets 5 years' jail, caning for assaulting student during e-hailing ride
Article:
TAWAU: A travel agent was sentenced to five years' jail and two strokes of the rotan by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to physically sexually assaulting a public university student three years ago.
The incident occurred while he was transporting the victim to her destination using an e-hailing service.
Judge Ahmad Faizadh Yahaya handed down the sentence after accepting the guilty plea from Mohd Shahremy Abdul Raset, 31.
According to the charge, the man committed the sexual act against the victim, who was 17 years old at the time, inside a Perodua Myvi car.
The offence took place during a journey from Balung River Eco Resort to Shanshui Resort at Batu 9, Jalan Apas, near here, between 7.15pm and 7.22pm on Dec 9, 2023.
He was charged under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, which carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Based on the facts of the case, the victim had contacted the accused for an e-hailing service, during which the accused took advantage of the situation to sexually assault her during the journey.
The accused, who was unrepresented, pleaded for a lenient sentence on the grounds that he was the sole breadwinner for his family, supporting a non-working wife and two children aged two and four.
However, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Haikal Hazwan Md Yatin urged the court to impose a heavy and deterrent sentence, taking into account the severity of the crime and public interest.
The court ordered the prison sentence to take effect from July 3 and directed the accused to undergo rehabilitation counselling during his imprisonment under Section 26 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
The accused was also ordered to be placed under police supervision for two years upon completing his prison term, in accordance with Section 27 of the same act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US reiterates backing for Tibetan aspirations to preserve culture
Article:
WASHINGTON: The US State Department said on Wednesday it will continue to call on China to return to dialogue with the Dalai Lama, after a Tibetan man set himself on fire near UN headquarters last week.
The Tibetan man died a week ago from severe burns suffered near the United Nations headquarters in New York.
Activists and a media outlet of exiled Tibetans identified the man as Lobga Rangzen.
Voice of Tibet said he "self-immolated outside the UN headquarters in New York after a live appeal for Tibetan independence and unity."
"The United States is committed to supporting the unalienable human rights and aspirations of Tibetans to celebrate and preserve their unique culture, language, and religion without fear of interference," a State Department spokesperson said in a statement.
"The United States will continue to call on China to return to direct dialogue, without pre-conditions, with the Dalai Lama and his representatives, and with the democratically elected Tibetan leaders, to resolve differences and achieve meaningful autonomy for Tibetans."
Washington has long said it supports Tibetan human rights, across both Republican and Democratic administrations.
"We urge the US side to honour its commitments to recognise Tibet as a part of China and not support Tibet independence, and to stop using Tibet-related issues to interfere in China's internal affairs," said Mao Ning, spokesperson for China's Foreign Ministry, at a regular news conference on Thursday.
China said after the man's death that Tibet has been an inalienable part of the country's territory since ancient times and Beijing believes "relevant countries will handle the matter in accordance with domestic laws."
Beijing has previously said Washington was in "no position" to point fingers at China on Tibet-related issues.
China, which seeks to consolidate its control over Tibet, regards the Dalai Lama as a separatist and says it must approve his successor, citing a centuries-old ritual.
The Dalai Lama, who is the spiritual head of Tibetan Buddhists and a Nobel Peace Prize winner, has urged his followers to reject anyone chosen by Beijing.
China seized control of Tibet in 1950 in what it describes as a "peaceful liberation" from feudalistic serfdom.
International human-rights groups and exiles have routinely condemned what they call China's oppressive rule in Tibetan areas. China rejects such assessments.
Ethnic minority issues are highly sensitive in China, with Tibetans and other minorities put under heavy surveillance for any sign of alleged "separatism."
The International Campaign for Tibet says there have been 159 self-immolations by Tibetans in Tibet since 2009.
According to its data, 11 self-immolations by Tibetans occurred while people were in exile.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia will only import oil from Russia if acceptable from an international trading basis, minister says
Article:
SINGAPORE: Malaysia will only import oil from Russia if it is "acceptable from an international trading basis", Second Finance Minister Amir Hamzah Azizan said at the Reuters NEXT Asia conference in Singapore on Thursday.
Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim's state visit to Russia and Turkmenistan in June yielded new energy commitments, according to state media Bernama.
Russia assured Malaysia supplies of petrol, oil, and gas, while in Turkmenistan, Malaysia's state-owned energy firm Petronas signed several new agreements, including collaborations with state companies Turkmennebit and Hazarnebit, to strengthen its presence in the Caspian Sea and expand its upstream portfolio.
Malaysia continues to maintain trade and diplomatic relations with Russia and is not part of the Western sanctions imposed on the country. View the live broadcast of the World Stage and read full coverage of the summit here.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Witness says Lian instructed letters seeking Jana Wibawa projects
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Businessman Lian Tian Chuan, better known as TC Lian, instructed his subordinate to prepare letters to then prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin seeking government projects under the Jana Wibawa programme, the High Court heard today.
Nepturis Sdn Bhd director and senior executive procurement Aliza Abd Malek said Lian personally decided the titles of the letters and instructed her to prepare applications for the Klang Utara district police headquarters project and the Central Spine Road (CSR) project in 2021.
However, Aliza said she did not know who the letters were eventually submitted to or how they reached Muhyiddin, as the matter was handled entirely by Lian.
Reading from her witness statement, Aliza said she prepared the letters in February and March 2021 before handing them to Nepturis managing director Rizman Abdullah for his signature.
"After the letters were signed, I returned them to Lian together with A4-sized envelopes.
"I do not know who the letters were submitted to or who Lian dealt with to forward them to Muhyiddin.
"Lian never told me who he was dealing with regarding the applications," she said before judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin.
Aliza was testifying in Muhyiddin's trial, where the former prime minister faces four charges of using his position as prime minister and Bersatu president to obtain RM232.5 million in alleged gratification for the party, as well as three money laundering charges involving RM200 million.
The witness told the court that she and Rizman became Nepturis directors and equal shareholders in 2018 after being asked by Lian to take over the company.
Aliza said Lian held no official position or shares in Nepturis, but they understood that he effectively had a 30 per cent interest in the company through funding provided by his company, Metrasys Sdn Bhd.
She said major decisions involving Nepturis required Lian's approval.
Aliza said Nepturis only became aware it was being considered under the Jana Wibawa programme after receiving an email from the Finance Ministry on March 26, 2021.
Following the notification, Nepturis submitted the required documents to the ministry before being informed by the Public Works Department that it had passed the pre-qualification stage and was invited to submit a tender.
She said the department later issued a letter of intent on Dec 10, 2021, offering Nepturis the RM141 million contract to build the Klang Utara district police headquarters, before issuing the letter of acceptance on Jan 12, 2022.
It was revealed during the trial that Nepturis had obtained overdraft facilities from Maybank in February 2022, including a RM1 million payment made to Bersatu.
Lian's name has also featured in other corruption proceedings linked to the Jana Wibawa programme involving Muhyiddin's son-in-law Adam Radlan Adam Muhammad and former Bersatu information chief Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan.
In Adam Radlan's case, the former Segambut Bersatu division chief was charged with soliciting RM2 million from Lian as an inducement to help Nepturis secure the RM141 million project.
However, Adam Radlan was acquitted and discharged in August last year after the prosecution withdrew the case following his payment of a RM4.1 million compound.
Meanwhile, in a separate case, Wan Saiful was charged with soliciting a bribe from Lian as an inducement to help Nepturis secure the CSR construction project from Sertik to the East Coast Expressway interchange.
He was alleged to have committed the offence at the Royal Lake Club, Taman Tasik Perdana, in April 2022.
The trial continues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Abuse cases handled by One Stop Crisis Centre hit five-year high, 12,906 victims in 2025
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The number of abuse and violence cases handled through the One Stop Crisis Centre (OSCC) climbed to 12,906 in 2025, compared with 9,652 cases in 2020.
Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Women, Children and Social Development deputy chairman Rodziah Ismail said the sharp increase, which reached a five-year high, reflected the growing scale of violence and abuse cases, proving that victim protection was a wider societal issue.
She said the matter should now be viewed as a public health, social protection, and community safety issue requiring coordinated and comprehensive action.
"Physical abuse still accounts for the highest number of cases, but the significant rise in sexual abuse cases, from 2,699 in 2020 to 4,258 in 2025, is a worrying trend.
"This shows that protection systems, early detection and intervention measures for victims of sexual violence must be strengthened urgently," she said during a briefing on improvements to the OSCC at the Dewan Rakyat today.
The Ampang member of parliament said female victims remained higher in number, with 10,176 cases in 2025 compared with 2,730 male victims, while sexual abuse cases involved 3,852 women and 406 men.
Rodziah said domestic violence was the largest category of OSCC cases in 2025, accounting for almost 60 per cent with 7,689 cases, while more than half involved spouses as perpetrators.
She said the committee's review of seven hospitals with OSCC facilities highlighted several gaps, including delays in police action, inconsistent responses, weaknesses in post-treatment victim protection, and shortages of Social Welfare Department protection officers at some hospitals and in several states.
The committee proposed 10 recommendations to improve OSCC services, covering areas such as dedicated annual funding for operations and infrastructure, specialised management positions within relevant agencies, and the swift implementation of OSCC guidelines and an integrated data system.
The committee called on the police force to clarify procedures for cross-district and cross-state cases, ensure timely police attendance after hospital reports, and strengthen specialist training for officers handling sexual violence, domestic violence, and child abuse cases.
Rodziah said the committee recommended amendments to the Child Act 2001, including the creation of dedicated assistant protection officer positions at hospitals with OSCC facilities, expanding the department's protection officer workforce, and improving case handling outside normal working hours.
She said awareness campaigns on domestic violence, sexual and child abuse, as well as the services provided by the OSCC, should be stepped up to ensure greater public understanding of the centre's role and accessibility.
"Our goal is not just to ensure victims receive hospital treatment, but to help them escape the cycle of abuse, access protection with dignity and begin their recovery journey in a safe environment," she said.
Speaking during the briefing session, Teresa Kok (PH-Seputeh) said the increase in cases reflected the ongoing need for OSCC services, calling for government monitoring to include assessments of intervention outcomes rather than focusing solely on statistics, and to address psychological care, protection measures, and repeat cases of violence.
Dr Siti Mastura Muhammad (PN-Kepala Batas) urged the government to strengthen family institutions through a lifelong approach, including reviving the Lelaki Qawwam module to promote responsible male leadership.
She said post-marriage courses should be expanded to include counselling for young couples, regular household management sessions, and targeted support for high-risk families.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippines rejects Chinese scholars' claim over its island province near Taiwan
Article:
MANILA: The defence minister of the Philippines on Thursday rejected as "baseless" and "ludicrous" assertions by Chinese scholars that its northernmost island-chain province belongs to Beijing, calling the claim concerning and worthy of challenge.
Chinese state-run news site GDToday reported on July 2 that scholars from institutions including Nanjing University argued at a June 30 symposium that Batanes was a natural extension of Taiwan and therefore belonged to China.
Beijing has not formally endorsed that position.
The assertions may add a new dimension to long-running tensions between Manila and Beijing, which are already embroiled in multiple disputes over islands and features in the South China Sea.
"I view this, once again, as probably a signal of a preconceived intention," Defence Secretary Gilberto Teodoro told reporters.
"It is not far-fetched to think that this is already part of their plan. And it also validates what we have been saying that they have a plan to control the entire Pacific Ocean."
"What is this for, right? And we know this is baseless. This is nonsense. It is ludicrous," he said. "So this is concerning, and it is something that must be challenged," he added, but did not elaborate.
The Chinese embassy in Manila did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment on Teodoro's remarks.
Batanes, home to about 20,000 people, is about 160 km (100 miles) south of Taiwan along the strategically important Luzon Strait, a key passage linking the South China Sea and the Pacific Ocean.
It has become increasingly important in security planning and has hosted joint military exercises involving Philippine and allied US forces.
Beijing previously sanctioned Teodoro and his close relatives over what it said were "erroneous remarks" made about China.
The scholars' comments came weeks after the Philippines and Japan announced in May they would begin formal talks on delimiting the maritime boundary of their exclusive economic zones and continental shelves in accordance with international law, a move China criticised.
Beijing claims almost the entire South China Sea, a strategic waterway through which more than US$3 trillion in trade passes annually, despite a 2016 arbitral ruling that invalidated those claims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Tiram needs solutions, not experiments, says BN candidate
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional's Tiram candidate Senator Datuk Halim Sulaimen has urged voters not to repeat what he described as the "emotion-driven" decision made during the 14th General Election (GE14).
BN suffered a major setback in the 2018 polls, losing federal power for the first time in history, with voters turning against the coalition amid widespread dissatisfaction over governance issues, cost of living pressures and public sentiment.
Halim warned that such choices could result in electing representatives who fail to deliver meaningful change.
The Johor Umno secretary said voters should evaluate candidates based on their track record, ability and commitment to serve the constituency rather than being influenced by temporary sentiments.
He said the outcome of GE14 showed that changes in political preference did not necessarily translate into improvements on the ground over the following five years.
"For BN, we have always been rooted here in Johor. We are locals and understand the responsibilities needed to address the concerns of residents.
"I am a son of this area, I grew up here and understand the problems and needs of the people of Tiram," he said.
Halim said his campaign was centred on three key priorities — improving infrastructure, expanding economic opportunities and preserving the social fabric of the constituency.
He said worsening traffic congestion had become a major concern due to rapid development in the area, requiring better planning and immediate intervention.
"Traffic congestion is happening because development has moved faster. Action needs to be taken to address this issue," he said.
On economic empowerment, Halim said he aimed to help Tiram residents achieve higher incomes, including through Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programmes.
He said the target of achieving RM3,000 monthly wages among local residents should be pursued through skills development, especially as many residents currently work in Singapore.
"We need to look at how Tiram residents can achieve this income level, including through TVET and education," he said.
Halim also stressed the importance of preserving local culture and traditions as part of maintaining the constituency's identity.
He said his previous two-term experience as Puteri Wangsa assemblyman would provide him with an advantage in understanding community issues and delivering solutions.
"The priority is ensuring continuity of quality service for the people.
"The choice is in the hands of voters. Choose a candidate who has proven he can work," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anutin wishes Anwar luck ahead of Johor polls
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has wished Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim success in the upcoming Johor election this Saturday.
Anutin extended his well-wishes to the Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman at the conclusion of their joint press conference held in conjunction with his two-day official visit to Malaysia.
"I wish you the best of luck for the upcoming election in Johor," he said.
The Johor election on Saturday will see 172 candidates contesting for 56 state assembly seats, with both Barisan Nasional (BN) and PH fielding candidates in all contested constituencies.
Meanwhile, Anwar described his meeting with Anutin as warm and productive.
"Please come again to Malaysia because you make us feel so comfortable, happy, and at peace. This kind of relationship does not happen with many countries; it happens because Thailand is a very important country to Malaysia," Anwar said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Myanmar crisis best resolved through inclusive engagement
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia believes the Myanmar crisis can only be resolved through inclusive engagement with all stakeholders while allowing its people to determine their own future, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Speaking at a joint press conference with Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, Anwar said Malaysia values Thailand's views on the issue, given its position as Myanmar's neighbour and a key regional partner.
"Malaysia believes the best way to resolve this long-standing issue is through effective engagement and by allowing the people of Myanmar to determine their own future. Thailand, as a neighbour and close friend, has a very important role in this effort," he said.
Anwar also welcomed the Thai foreign minister's initiative to convene Asean foreign ministers to exchange views on the crisis.
On bilateral security, Anwar said Malaysia appreciated Thailand's commitment to maintaining peace along the two countries' shared border.
"We will continue to hold discussions and exchange views to prevent any escalation of tensions. We believe in the wisdom of both countries' leadership to ensure Asean remains peaceful, stable and dynamic," he said.
Anutin, who is on a two-day official visit to Malaysia, thanked Anwar for Malaysia's firm stance against violence and its commitment to ensuring those responsible are brought to justice.
He said both countries had also agreed to strengthen cooperation against transnational crime, including establishing a joint task force to coordinate security along the Golok River.
"The joint task force will coordinate security challenges along the Golok River. We also agreed to strengthen cooperation between the relevant authorities to improve water-level monitoring and enhance flood prevention measures in the Golok River basin," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar says Malaysia must move beyond race, religion debates amid new security threats
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed concern over the growing use of state, racial and religious sentiments, warning that such divisions could threaten national harmony and security if left unchecked.
He said Malaysians should not become distracted by old political polemics or divisive identity-based narratives, as the country now faces more complex security challenges, including technological and digital threats.
“I often remind my colleagues during parliamentary debates that we are still dwelling on old issues - race, religion, regional and state sentiments.
“These are not the issues that deserve our greatest attention when the country is facing much bigger threats. That is what concerns me,” he said when officiating the National Security Month 2026 programme organised by the National Security Council (MKN) today.
Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and National Security director-general Datuk Raja Nurshirwan Zainal Abidin.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said leaders across government departments, agencies and ministries must adopt a more proactive approach instead of waiting for circumstances to change before responding to increasingly complex security threats.
“This is the responsibility of leaders in every department, agency and ministry. This is not routine bureaucracy. We must quickly understand emerging technologies and new security challenges,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Deputy minister: 81pc of Klang Valley workers report improved job performance under flexible work arrangements
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — A study on the impact of the flexible work arrangements policy by the Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma) has found that 81 per cent of workers in the Klang Valley reported improved job performance, clear evidence that work flexibility drives productivity.
Deputy Human Resources Minister Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the findings came from a study conducted across the Klang Valley, Johor and Penang to evaluate how effectively the arrangement is working for both employees and employers.
In Johor, he said 77 per cent of workers were more productive when given the freedom to set their own start and end times, while 64.4 per cent said working from home made their jobs easier.
In Penang, 77 per cent of employers reported a noticeable boost in operational efficiency after adopting the flexible work arrangements.
“The policy not only lifts worker productivity but also sharpens labour market efficiency, cuts commuting costs including fuel and public transport fares and promotes a healthier work-life balance,” he said in the Dewan Rakyat’s special chamber session today.
Khairul Firdaus was replying to Datuk Mumtaz Md Nawi (PN-Tumpat), who had raised questions over the effectiveness of the work-from-home and flexible work arrangements policies.
He stressed that the arrangements enable more individuals, especially women, parents, caregivers, senior citizens who remain productive, and those with other commitments, to stay in or re-join the workforce, directly supporting the government’s push to boost labour force participation.
Under amendments to the Employment Act 1955 that came into force on Jan 1, 2023, private sector workers have the right to apply for flexible work arrangements under Sections 60P and 60Q, covering flexible hours, work days, or work locations, including working from home, subject to the employer’s approval.
To push more companies on board, he said the government offers a 50 per cent tax deduction incentive for employers who implement such arrangements, covering employee training costs and software purchases to support digital work transformation.
“This one-off incentive up to RM500,000 can be claimed through TalentCorp for assessment years 2025 to 2027, subject to terms and conditions,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Police sergeant begins 14-month jail term after Court of Appeal upholds bribery conviction
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — A police sergeant began serving a 14-month jail sentence today after the Court of Appeal dismissed his appeal against his conviction and sentence for accepting bribes, while allowing the prosecution’s cross-appeal to reinstate an earlier conviction on a separate charge.
A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Noorin Badaruddin, Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin and Datuk Meor Hashimi Abdul Hamid unanimously dismissed Mohd Ridhuan Kasah’s appeal against his conviction and sentence on the second charge.
The court also allowed the prosecution’s cross-appeal, setting aside the High Court’s decision to discharge and acquit the 48-year-old on the first charge and reinstating the Sessions Court’s conviction and 12-month jail sentence.
However, Mohd Ridhuan will serve 14 months’ imprisonment as both jail terms are to run concurrently.
He was also ordered to pay a total fine of RM20,000 or face an additional 12 months’ imprisonment in default.
The court ordered Mohd Ridhuan to begin serving his sentence immediately. He had previously been granted a stay of execution of his sentence and was released on RM20,000 bail with one surety pending the disposal of his appeal.
Delivering the unanimous judgment, Justice Noorin said the High Court had erred in acquitting Mohd Ridhuan on the first charge but was correct in upholding his conviction on the second charge.
She said the prosecution had adduced clear, consistent and credible evidence that proved every essential element of the first charge beyond a reasonable doubt.
On the second charge, she said Mohd Ridhuan’s defence amounted to a bare denial and failed to offer any reasonable explanation to rebut the prosecution’s case.
Mohd Ridhuan was charged with two counts of accepting RM1,000 in bribes from Syed Mohd Firdaus Syed Hussin at the Criminal Investigation Department office of the Pekan district police headquarters in Pahang. The money was allegedly offered as an inducement for him not to take legal action against the complainant.
The offences were allegedly committed at 5 pm on Sept 18, 2020, and at 12.30 pm the following day.
On Feb 7, 2024, the Sessions Court convicted Mohd Ridhuan on both charges and sentenced him to 12 months’ imprisonment and a RM10,000 fine, or six months’ imprisonment in default, on the first charge, and 14 months’ imprisonment and a RM10,000 fine, or six months’ imprisonment in default, on the second charge.
The jail terms were ordered to run concurrently.
On Nov 3 last year, the High Court acquitted him on the first charge but upheld his conviction and sentence on the second charge.
At today’s proceedings, lawyers Mohammad Hissham Ramdzan, Mohamad Hamizey Mat Taib and Halimaton Saadiah Abu Samah represented Mohd Ridhuan, while the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission. Deputy Public Prosecutors Farah Ezlin Yusof Khan, Dr Ifa Sirrhu Samsudin, Nidzuwan Abd Latip and Atirah Mohd Azuarizanuddin appeared for the prosecution. — Bernama

Title: Anwar: Malaysia must move beyond conventional security methods to tackle AI, drone threats
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 9 — Malaysia must shift from conventional security methods to a more comprehensive strategy to address rapidly evolving threats, including emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), post-quantum cryptography and drones, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said the changing security landscape required close collaboration between the government, private sector and the public to ensure the country’s security remained safeguarded.
“A whole-of-nation approach is essential. There can no longer be compartmentalisation where issues are seen as belonging solely to the private sector, a department or a ministry.
“We must foster synergy through a coordinated approach involving government departments and ministries,” he said when launching National Security Month 2026, organised by the National Security Council (MKN), here today.
Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and National Security director-general Datuk Raja Nurshirwan Zainal Abidin.
At the same event, Anwar also launched the National Cyber Security Summit (NCSS) 2026, the National Cryptography Policy (MyKriptografi), the MyKriptografi Action Plan and the AI Systems Cybersecurity Framework (AISCF).
He said safeguarding national security was no longer the sole responsibility of the military, police and enforcement agencies, but a collective responsibility that must be shared by all segments of society.
“We have moved beyond the conventional understanding of security, where it was seen solely as the responsibility of the military, the police and other enforcement agencies.
“Today, security concerns have reached every home and every individual,” he said.
Anwar said families should serve as the first line of defence in protecting children from harmful values and ideologies that could undermine national security.
“Just as digital systems have firewalls, every family should build its own firewall to safeguard not only the morals and values of children but also protect them from being influenced by ideologies that could threaten national security,” he said.
The prime minister said the government had also introduced the National Cryptography Policy, the MyKriptografi Action Plan and Malaysia’s post-quantum cryptography sandbox programme to prepare the country for rapid technological advances.
He added that close cooperation with friendly countries remained essential in addressing cross-border threats, including cybersecurity, border security and the use of emerging technologies.
“If we do not have close cooperation with other countries, we will face difficulties,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Perlis MB expects tourism boost as Kuala Perlis-Satun ferry resumes after Covid-19 hiatus
Article:
KANGAR, July 9 — Perlis expects tourist arrivals to reach between 5.5 million and six million this year following the resumption of the Kuala Perlis-Satun ferry service today, which is expected to further boost the state’s tourism sector.
Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah said the reopening of the sea route, suspended since the Covid-19 pandemic, would facilitate the movement of tourists between Satun and Kuala Perlis while generating economic spillover for local communities and contributing to the state’s economy.
“I believe Kuala Perlis will also receive more tourists from Thailand to shop here, which will help increase state revenue while strengthening ties between Malaysia and Thailand, particularly Perlis and Satun,” he told reporters after welcoming a delegation from Satun at the Kuala Perlis Ferry Terminal today.
Abu Bakar said the ferry service is also expected to ease congestion at the Padang Besar border crossing, especially during public holidays, as travellers will have the option of using the sea route between Kuala Perlis and Satun.
The journey between Kuala Perlis and Satun takes about 40 minutes, offering visitors an alternative travel experience with scenic coastal views, he added.
Meanwhile, Abu Bakar said the state government plans to install a floating pontoon to address berthing difficulties caused by fluctuating water levels and shallow waters at the ferry terminal.
He added that the state government is also exploring the reactivation of the Kuala Perlis-Satun Roll-on/Roll-off (Ro-Ro) service and is in discussions with several investors on a proposed RM500 million bridge extending about two kilometres into the sea to overcome persistent shallow navigation channel issues. — Bernama

Title: DPMM welcomes digital foreign worker quota system, calls for continued improvements
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 9 — The Malay Chamber of Commerce Malaysia (DPMM) hopes the digital foreign worker quota application system via the e-Quota module in the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) will be monitored and enhanced to keep the approval process efficient, transparent, and responsive to industry needs.
DPMM President Norsyahrin Hamidon said system stability, processing times, procedural clarity, and employer support must be closely monitored to achieve the objectives of foreign worker management reform.
“DPMM hopes the implementation of this new system will be constantly monitored and improved to keep the approval process efficient, transparent, and responsive to industry needs,” he said in a statement today.
On July 6, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan announced that all foreign worker quota applications are now processed entirely through the e-Quota module in the FWCMS, effectively ending case-by-case approvals.
